title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Rich, creamy, and flavorful – perfect for a satisfying lunch or dinner.
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani step by step
best homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, garnished with fresh cilantro and served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian Murgh Makhani dish perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a globally loved Indian classic featuring succulent pieces of chicken simmered in a rich, velvety tomato and cashew-based gravy. This dish is renowned for its mild yet complex flavors, beautifully balanced with the sweetness of tomatoes and the richness of butter and cream. It offers a truly comforting and luxurious experience, perfect for a satisfying lunch or a special dinner.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, and sear for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned. The chicken does not need to be cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, soaked and pureed cashews (blend cashews with a little water until smooth), remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and oil separates.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, bay leaf) if desired for a smoother texture. You can also blend the gravy with an immersion blender or transfer to a regular blender (be careful with hot liquids) for an extra smooth consistency. Add the seared chicken back into the gravy. Stir well to coat the chicken.

instructions.title: Finish with Cream and Butter
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and the remaining 1 tbsp of butter. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on low heat, just until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is heated through. Do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if needed. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

tips: For an extra rich and smooth gravy, blend the cooked tomato-cashew base with an immersion blender or in a regular blender until completely smooth before adding the chicken.
Always marinate your chicken for at least 30 minutes; longer marination (up to 4 hours) in the refrigerator will yield more tender and flavorful chicken.

Adjust the level of spice to your preference. For more heat, add a pinch more red chili powder or a finely chopped green chili during the onion sautéing step. For less heat, reduce the red chili powder.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's a culinary icon, a creamy, dreamy testament to the rich flavors of Indian cuisine. Renowned globally for its luscious gravy and tender chicken, this beloved recipe is a cornerstone of Indian lunch tables, special occasions, and takeout menus alike. If you’ve ever yearned for a meal that warms the soul and delights the palate, look no further than the magic of Murgh Makhani. This article will guide you through the journey of creating an authentic and unforgettable Butter Chicken experience right in your own kitchen, perfect for a satisfying midday meal.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi, translates literally to "chicken with butter." Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant, this dish was serendipitously created from leftover tandoori chicken immersed in a rich tomato-based gravy. The result was a revolutionary dish that quickly captured the hearts of food lovers worldwide. The defining characteristics of Murgh Makhani are its incredibly tender pieces of chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled or pan-fried, swimming in a velvety, slightly sweet, and tangy tomato gravy. This gravy is generously enriched with butter and cream, balanced by a subtle blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and sometimes a hint of ginger and garlic. It’s the ultimate comfort food, a dish that perfectly balances richness with delicate spice, making it an ever-popular choice among Indian lunch recipes.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a harmonious blend of fresh ingredients and aromatic spices. Here’s what you’ll need to gather:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
* 500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast), cut into 1-inch pieces
* 1/2 cup plain yogurt
* 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
* 1 teaspoon red chili powder (preferably Kashmiri for color)
* 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
* 1 teaspoon garam masala
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1 tablespoon lemon juice

**For the Gravy:**
* 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
* 1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
* 1 large onion, finely chopped
* 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
* 1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 fresh large tomatoes, pureed
* 1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional but recommended for creaminess)
* 1/2 cup heavy cream (or whipping cream)
* 1 tablespoon sugar (adjust to taste for balance)
* 1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
* Salt to taste
* Fresh cilantro for garnish

**Whole Spices (for tempering):**
* 2-3 green cardamom pods
* 2-3 cloves
* 1 small cinnamon stick
* 1 bay leaf

**Powdered Spices:**
* 1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri)
* 1 teaspoon coriander powder
* 1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
* 1/2 teaspoon garam masala

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create your own delectable Butter Chicken:

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken.** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor and tenderness.

**Step 2: Cook the Chicken.** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook in batches if necessary. Sear the chicken until it's golden brown and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Gravy Base.** In the same pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon stick, bay leaf) and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped onions and cook until they turn translucent and light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

**Step 4: Build the Gravy Flavors.** Add the crushed tomatoes (or pureed fresh tomatoes) to the pan along with the soaked cashews (if using). Stir in all the powdered spices (red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala) and a pinch of salt. Cook this mixture, stirring occasionally, for about 10-15 minutes, or until the oil begins to separate from the sides of the pan. This step is crucial for developing a rich, deep flavor.

**Step 5: Blend and Strain the Gravy.** Remove the bay leaf and cinnamon stick. Let the gravy base cool slightly, then carefully transfer it to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky, restaurant-style texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid. Discard any remaining solids.

**Step 6: Finish the Gravy.** Return the strained gravy to the pot and place it over low heat. Stir in the heavy cream, sugar, and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer gently for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the gravy to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if needed.

**Step 7: Combine and Serve.** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken evenly. Let it simmer for another 5 minutes to ensure the chicken absorbs the rich flavors of the gravy. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken is easier with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The yogurt tenderizes the chicken, and the spices infuse it with flavor, making every bite succulent.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** This specific chili powder provides a beautiful, vibrant red color to your gravy without adding excessive heat. It's essential for the authentic look of Butter Chicken.
*   **Blend and Strain:** This step might seem like an extra effort, but blending and then straining the gravy base is crucial for achieving that incredibly smooth, luxurious texture that defines Murgh Makhani.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** The base of your gravy relies heavily on tomatoes. Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or ripe, flavorful fresh tomatoes for the best taste.
*   **Kasoori Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** This spice is non-negotiable! It imparts a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor that is characteristic of Murgh Makhani. Lightly toast them in a dry pan before crushing and adding to enhance their aroma.
*   **Balance Sweet and Tangy:** The sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. Adjust it to your preference, creating a harmonious sweet and tangy profile.
*   **Don't Skimp on Butter and Cream:** While you can modify for health, the richness from butter and cream is integral to the authentic taste and texture of this creamy Indian lunch recipe.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a perfect, fulfilling Indian lunch.

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, warm naan, especially garlic naan or butter naan, is ideal for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Aromatic basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a comforting base that beautifully complements the creamy chicken.
*   **Roti or Paratha:** For a lighter option, serve with whole wheat roti or flaky paratha.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple side salad of sliced cucumbers, onions, and tomatoes with a squeeze of lemon can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or mint) can also provide a pleasant counterpoint to the flavors.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is a masterpiece, you can explore variations to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this dish into Paneer Makhani by substituting the chicken with cubes of paneer (Indian cottage cheese), or Tofu Makhani for a vegan option. You can also make a Mixed Vegetable Makhani.
*   **Healthier Options:** To reduce calories, use lean chicken breast, reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some of the cream with low-fat yogurt (stirred in at the very end to prevent curdling). Grilling or baking the chicken also cuts down on oil.
*   **Spicier Version:** If you prefer more heat, add a finely chopped green chili along with the ginger-garlic paste, or increase the amount of red chili powder.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. To maintain thickness, you might add a bit more cream or a spoonful of almond flour, or thicken with a cornstarch slurry.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic tandoori flavor, you can smoke your cooked chicken using the *dhungar* method (placing a small, hot charcoal in the dish with some ghee and covering it briefly).

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a truly magnificent dish, offering a symphony of flavors and textures that tantalize the senses. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore Indian cuisine, mastering this recipe will bring immense satisfaction. It’s a dish that brings warmth, comfort, and joy to any table, making it an ideal, hearty, and universally loved Indian lunch recipe. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to embark on a delicious culinary adventure that will leave everyone asking for more. Enjoy your homemade Murgh Makhani!
